description: Document structure template for debate-prism proposal generation (Step 6). Decision-focused narrative; disclaimer stamped when claim touches finance/legal/medical.

sections.heading: ## Disclaimer
sections.condition: stamp when claim or entity anchors contain finance/legal/medical keywords (finance, legal, medical, investment, securities, ticker, options, lawsuit, litigation, clinical, diagnosis, treatment, prescription)
sections.content: Renders the standard disclaimer template from SKILL.md verbatim. Informational-only framing. Named securities sized as percentages of a risk budget, not dollar amounts.

sections.heading: ## Claim
sections.content: The full claim statement extracted in Step 1.

sections.heading: ## Research Plan
sections.content: Entity anchors, source classes, recency window, freshness class, authority preferences, fetch budget from Step 0.

sections.heading: ## Baseline
sections.content: The primary agent's one-paragraph initial read with its single grounding citation. Establishes the reference point against which the adversaries are measured.

subsections.heading: ### Core Position
subsections.content: One-to-two-sentence core position from the for-advocate.

subsections.heading: ### Citations
subsections.content: Table: index | title | url | sourceClass | one-line excerpt. Full citation entries conform to citation-schema.json.

subsections.heading: ### Analysis Summary
subsections.content: Step-by-step analysis with inline citation-index references. 3–8 bullets.

subsections.heading: ### For-Recommendation
subsections.content: What the for-advocate recommends given only the for-case.

subsections.heading: ### Citations
subsections.content: Table: index | title | url | sourceClass | one-line excerpt. ZERO URL overlap with for-brief citations (mechanically enforced). If citationOverlap='unresolved' was tagged, include the warning note here.

subsections.heading: ### Targeted Citation
subsections.content: If targetedCitationIndex is non-null, name the for-advocate citation this case directly rebuts. Otherwise: 'General counter-case; no single for-citation targeted.'

subsections.heading: ### Analysis Summary
subsections.content: Step-by-step analysis with inline citation-index references.

subsections.heading: ### Against-Recommendation
subsections.content: What the against-advocate recommends given only the counter-case.

subsections.heading: ### Weakening Evidence
subsections.content: Either the named against-citation (index + excerpt) that most weakened the for-case, OR 'None' with justification. Schema-enforced non-empty.

subsections.heading: ### Verdict
subsections.content: endorse | reject | revise. If revise, state the revised claim.

subsections.heading: ### Flip Conditions
subsections.content: What evidence would flip the verdict (or reject the revised claim).

subsections.heading: ### Confidence
subsections.content: high | medium | low, with one-line rationale.

subsections.heading: ### Degradation Flags
subsections.content: List of degradation warnings surfaced in earlier steps (citationOverlap='unresolved', degradationEvidence='unverified', recency-gate warnings). Empty when none.

sections.heading: ## Round History
sections.condition: present when rounds > 1 (either judge demanded round-two OR --round-two flag was set)
sections.content: Summary of the round-two for-brief (core position + new citations + how it addressed the targetedCitationIndex) and the judge's second pass verdict.
